briefing.opening: Good morning. I will now begin the climate briefing for March 19, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Climate Crisis: Domestic and International Vulnerabilities and Urgent Calls for Action
sections.content: The direct impacts of the climate crisis and the urgent need for action are becoming increasingly prominent both domestically and internationally. In Korea, safety issues due to heatwaves and tropical nights, as well as the crisis facing local festivals like cherry blossom festivals, are becoming a reality [5, 6]. There are also urgent calls for measures to secure agricultural water [7]. Internationally, research has been published indicating that climate change can affect microorganisms, with implications for all life on Earth [62], and African health systems are facing climate change as a significant health crisis [63, 76]. In particular, warnings that a top-category storm like tropical cyclone 'Narel' will hit northern Queensland clearly demonstrate the severity of extreme weather events caused by climate change [89, 91].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Accelerating Domestic Carbon Neutrality Transition and Industry Efforts
sections.content: Domestic industries and local governments are accelerating concrete efforts towards a carbon-neutral society. The achievement of an 'A-' rating by the only Korean steel company to do so, and Continental's 'A-' rating in the CDP Climate Change Assessment, demonstrate companies' sustainable management performance [1, 2]. Notably, Gumi City is actively pursuing its transformation into the 'first model industrial complex for carbon neutrality' through a national project valued at 130.2 billion to 150 billion KRW [9, 10, 13, 14, 17, 18, 20, 25, 32, 34, 37, 38, 42, 43]. In Ulsan, the National Carbon Neutrality Science Center has broken ground, marking the first step in establishing a science hub for future generations [15, 16, 19, 21, 23, 28, 29, 35, 36]. Efforts to implement carbon neutrality are expanding across various local governments, including Gwacheon City's establishment of a regional energy plan reflecting carbon neutrality policies [11, 12, 26, 41] and Bucheon City's citizen-participatory carbon neutrality volunteer fair [22, 40] [3, 4, 8, 24, 27, 30, 31, 33, 39].

sections.title: 🌱 Expansion and Challenges of Renewable Energy Transition
sections.content: Globally, the transition to renewable energy is accelerating, accompanied by technological advancements and policy support. As public renewable energy and coal phase-out package legislation gains momentum [44], Europe is showing resilience amidst soaring energy prices by reducing gas consumption and expanding renewable energy [45]. Domestically, Siheung City is leading the expansion of renewable energy supply by promoting RE100 adoption in industrial complexes and constructing solar power facilities [46, 53, 55]. Expectations for Korea's energy transition are high, with companies like GS Entec showcasing their offshore wind capabilities at Japanese exhibitions [49, 50]. A 100 GW solar project in Indonesia is drawing attention as a potential catalyst for Southeast Asia's energy transition [54, 56], and market interest is growing, with the value of solar and wind stocks rising due to the impact of the Iran conflict [57]. However, challenges remain, such as the argument that renewable energy policies should be redesigned to prioritize local community benefits [47] and difficulties in achieving renewable energy transition targets in Africa [51].

sections.title: 🌍 International Climate Policy Conflicts and Resilience Building
sections.content: In the international community, alongside policy efforts to address climate change, fierce legal battles are unfolding to prevent the rollback of existing policies. In the United States, several states, including 24 states and Washington D.C., are actively challenging and filing lawsuits against moves by the Trump administration's Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to abandon its authority to address climate change or relax greenhouse gas regulations [59, 60, 61, 67, 73, 74, 75, 80, 87, 90]. These legal responses are considered crucial efforts to protect the foundation of climate change regulations. Meanwhile, Northern Ireland has announced an updated plan to enhance its resilience to climate change [68, 88], emphasizing the importance of climate adaptation. However, the absence of climate change mentions at the 2026 Oscar Awards [66] and the UK's decision to cut support for overseas climate action and divert funds to military spending [82] suggest that international attention and action on the climate change agenda remain imbalanced.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlighted the severity of the climate crisis, along with various response efforts underway both domestically and internationally. Moving forward, we must continue to effectively combat climate change through sustained interest and action. This concludes the climate briefing for March 19, 2026.

articles.title: Government to Provide National Subsidies in Digital Currency
articles.original_title: 정부 국고보조금 디지털화폐로 준다
articles.description: The government will, for the first time globally, disburse national subsidies in digital currency, utilizing 'deposit tokens' for a 30 billion KRW electric vehicle charging infrastructure project. This pilot program aims to provide subsidies to businesses installing EV charging facilities in locations such as parking lots and large supermarkets, using deposit tokens based on the Bank of Korea's digital currency. The Ministry of Economy and Finance, Ministry of Climate, Energy and Environment, and the Bank of Korea on the 2nd...

summary.phenomenon: The government is launching a pilot project, for the first time globally, to provide national subsidies for a 30 billion KRW electric vehicle charging infrastructure project using 'deposit tokens' based on the Bank of Korea's digital currency.
summary.outlook: This pilot project is planned to proceed by providing subsidies to businesses that intend to install electric vehicle charging facilities in locations such as parking lots and large supermarkets.

articles.description: Lawsuit says rescission of endangerment finding – which ruled greenhouse gases threaten public health – was illegalSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxA coalition of 24 states, alongside a dozen cities and counties, has sued the Trump administration over its decision to revoke the bedrock scientific determination underpinning virtually all US climate regulations.The new lawsuit, filed in the US Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit on Thur

summary.phenomenon: A coalition of 24 states, along with a dozen cities and counties, has sued the Trump administration over its decision to revoke the bedrock scientific determination (the endangerment finding, which ruled that greenhouse gases threaten public health) underpinning virtually all US climate regulations.
summary.cause: The cause is the Trump administration's decision to rescind the endangerment finding, which ruled that greenhouse gases threaten public health.
summary.outlook: The lawsuit claims that the rescission of this endangerment finding was illegal and aims to overturn the Trump administration's decision.

articles.title: ‘Damage will be widespread’: Narelle brings 250km/h wind gusts, with tourists evacuated and schools closed
articles.description: Massive storm tracking a path to Queensland coast, which intensified offshore Thursday morning to category 5, fuelled by warm waters in Coral SeaLive updates: Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle – latest newsCyclone Narelle path tracking mapSevere Tropical Cyclone Narelle is expected to make landfall in far north Queensland on Friday morning as a monster category 5 storm, bringing destructive wind gusts of 250km/h, according to the Bureau of Meteorology.The severe cyclone rapidly intensified over th

summary.phenomenon: Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle intensified to Category 5 offshore on Thursday morning and is expected to make landfall in far north Queensland on Friday morning as a Category 5 storm, bringing destructive wind gusts of 250 km/h.
summary.cause: The cyclone was fueled by warm waters in the Coral Sea.
summary.outlook: Widespread damage is anticipated due to this storm, and measures such as tourist evacuations and school closures have been implemented.
